assertion_results.text: A base exception class (AppError or similar) is defined with attributes: code, message, status_code, category, and support for __cause__ chaining
assertion_results.evidence: No base exception class defined. Service functions raise built-in ValueError and RuntimeError with string messages.

assertion_results.text: At least 4 domain-specific exception subclasses exist (e.g., NotFoundError, ValidationError, ServiceUnavailableError, PaymentError)
assertion_results.evidence: Only one custom exception defined: OrderNotFoundError(Exception). No ValidationError, ServiceUnavailableError, or PaymentError classes.

assertion_results.text: Exception wrapping uses 'raise NewError(...) from original_error' syntax to preserve the causal chain
assertion_results.evidence: No 'from' syntax used anywhere. Errors are raised as new exceptions without preserving the original: 'raise RuntimeError("Database error")' instead of 'raise ... from e'

assertion_results.text: No bare 'except:' or 'except Exception:' clauses that swallow errors without re-raising or wrapping
assertion_results.evidence: Found bare 'except Exception as e:' in process_payment that logs the error but returns None instead of re-raising, effectively swallowing the exception.

assertion_results.text: Structured logging outputs JSON-formatted log entries with at minimum: code, message, correlation_id, and timestamp
assertion_results.evidence: No structured logging. Uses print(f'Error: {e}') statements throughout. No JSON formatting, no correlation IDs, no timestamps.

assertion_results.text: Database errors (SQLAlchemy exceptions) are caught and wrapped in domain-specific errors with context
assertion_results.evidence: SQLAlchemy errors caught with bare 'except Exception' and re-raised as generic RuntimeError('Database error') with no domain context or original error preserved.

assertion_results.text: External API errors (httpx/HTTP errors) are caught and wrapped with the upstream service name and response details
assertion_results.evidence: httpx errors caught but wrapped as generic RuntimeError('API call failed'). No upstream service name, no response status code, no response body details.

assertion_results.text: Each error includes a stable string error code (e.g., ERR_ORDER_NOT_FOUND) not just a status number
assertion_results.evidence: No error codes used anywhere. Errors identified only by exception type and free-text messages.
